name: one_byte_sdu_frag20_no_ack
precondition: Freshly-initialized engine (Init(nullptr, false), mTxNextSeqNum starts at 0). Single HandleCharacteristicSend(sdu=[0x78], send_ack=false) call, fragment size 20.
note: packet_hex is the 1-byte SDU (input to fragmentation), not a wire packet. Output flags 0x05 = kStartMessage|kEndMessage (whole 1-byte SDU fits in one fragment: header 4 + payload 1 = 5 <= 20). seq 0x00 (first central tx). msgLen 0x0001 LE. payload 0x78. Verified by re-deriving BtpEngine.cpp's HandleCharacteristicSend algorithm in a standalone Python simulator.

name: one_byte_sdu_frag20_piggyback_ack1
precondition: Freshly-initialized engine with one unacked received fragment pending ack (mRxNewestUnackedSeqNum = 1). Single HandleCharacteristicSend(sdu=[0x78], send_ack=true) call, fragment size 20.
note: packet_hex is the 1-byte SDU (input). Output flags 0x0d = kStartMessage(0x01)|kFragmentAck(0x08)|kEndMessage(0x04). ack byte 0x01 (GetAndRecordRxAckSeqNum piggybacks pending rx ack). seq 0x00. msgLen 0x0001 LE. payload 0x78. Verified via Python simulation of BtpEngine.cpp's encode algorithm.

name: forty_byte_sdu_frag20
source: hand-encoded: chip:src/ble/BtpEngine.cpp:405-535 (HandleCharacteristicSend, Idle then two InProgress calls) + length assertions cross-checked against chip:src/ble/tests/TestBtpEngine.cpp:162-182 (HandleCharacteristicSendThreePacket, packet lengths 20/20/8)
precondition: Freshly-initialized engine (mTxNextSeqNum starts at 0). packet_hex is the 40-byte sequential SDU 0x00..0x27 (input). Three HandleCharacteristicSend calls: first with the full SDU and send_ack=false, then two with data=nullptr, send_ack=false, fragment size 20 (mirrors TestBtpEngine.cpp:162-182, which asserts packet0->DataLength() == 20, 20, then 8 after each call).
note: DISCREPANCY vs brief: brief stated the third (final) fragment's header flags as 0x04 (End only). Actual chip source (BtpEngine.cpp:499,514: `BitFlags<HeaderFlags> headerFlags(HeaderFlags::kContinueMessage)` is the base for every non-initial fragment, then `headerFlags.Set(HeaderFlags::kEndMessage)` ORs in the End bit -- it does not replace kContinueMessage) produces flags 0x06 (kContinueMessage|kEndMessage) for the final fragment of a multi-fragment TX message, not 0x04 alone. This differs from the RX-side test fixtures (e.g. HandleCharacteristicReceivedThreePacket) which hand-construct a bare 0x04 End-only final packet purely to test decoder acceptance (DidReceiveData/HasAny(Continue,End) accepts either) -- that is not what the real chip encoder emits. Chip source wins: final fragment here is flags 0x06, not 0x04. The brief also implied fragment 2's payload was 'first 16 payload bytes' followed immediately by fragment 3's 'final 6' with no gap -- confirmed correct after independently re-deriving the algorithm in Python (fragment1 payload = SDU[16:34), 18 bytes; fragment2 payload = SDU[34:40), 6 bytes). Byte breakdown: pkt0 = flags 0x01, seq 0x00, msgLen 0x0028 LE (40), payload = SDU[0:16) -- 4+16=20. pkt1 = flags 0x02 (kContinueMessage only, not last), seq 0x01, payload = SDU[16:34) (18 bytes) -- 2+18=20. pkt2 = flags 0x06 (kContinueMessage|kEndMessage), seq 0x02, payload = SDU[34:40) (6 bytes) -- 2+6=8.

name: forty_byte_sdu_frag20_midstream_ack
source: hand-encoded: chip:src/ble/BtpEngine.cpp:482-527 (HandleCharacteristicSend, kState_InProgress branch, send_ack piggyback on a mid fragment)
precondition: Same 40-byte sequential SDU (0x00..0x27, packet_hex above) and fragment size 20 as forty_byte_sdu_frag20. First HandleCharacteristicSend(sdu, send_ack=false) as before. Before the second call, a pending rx ack becomes available (mRxNewestUnackedSeqNum = 2); second call is HandleCharacteristicSend(nullptr, send_ack=true), piggybacking that ack onto fragment 2 (kTransferProtocolMidFragmentMaxHeaderSize = 3 bytes: flags+ack+seq, no msgLen on continuation fragments). Third call HandleCharacteristicSend(nullptr, send_ack=false) with no ack pending, completing the message.
note: pkt0 identical to forty_byte_sdu_frag20's first fragment (ack piggyback only starts at fragment 2): flags 0x01, seq 0x00, msgLen 0x0028 LE, payload = SDU[0:16). pkt1: flags 0x0a = kContinueMessage(0x02)|kFragmentAck(0x08), ack byte 0x02, seq 0x01, payload = SDU[16:33) (17 bytes) -- header 3 + payload 17 = 20 (recomputed split: mid-fragment header grows from 2 to 3 bytes when acking, so payload shrinks by 1 vs the no-ack split at this same offset). Remaining SDU after pkt1 = 40-16-17=7 bytes. pkt2: flags 0x06 = kContinueMessage|kEndMessage (per the same chip-source correction documented in forty_byte_sdu_frag20 -- the final fragment ORs End onto the Continue base, it does not replace it), seq 0x02, payload = SDU[33:40) (7 bytes) -- header 2 + payload 7 = 9. Every packet <= 20 bytes total as required. All four entries' output bytes were cross-checked with an independent Python re-implementation of BtpEngine.cpp's HandleCharacteristicSend algorithm.
